Bad Request

post.description:
If you want to cancel the content upload, the content id that is not currently available will not be uploaded.
Multi id can be set, and the appropriate contents id can be added. If the upload is successful, it will be included in the success list and if it is cancelled, it will be included in the cancellation list.
'successList' of the response data is List<String> type and this value consists of contentIds that was processed successfully.
'failList' of the response data is List<String> type and this value consists of contentIds that were not processed.

post.description:
Upload the content file to MagicINFO server.
Supports all file types required for creation, including images, videos, and web content package files.

parameters.name: contentType
parameters.description: Type of file to upload (example : IMAGE,MOVIE,OFFICE,PDF,FLASH,SOUND,HTML)

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: If you want to change web content package or SSSP Web App, input "UPDATE"(web content, SSSP Web App only)

parameters.name: refreshInterval
parameters.description: Refresh interval of web content. Format is "hh:mm:ss"(web content only, SSSP Web App only)

parameters.name: startPage
parameters.description: Start page name of web content such as "index.html"(web content only)

parameters.name: webContentName
parameters.description: Name of web content(web content only)

Internal Server Error

get.summary: Download thumbnail image of specified content
get.description:
Download thumbnail image of specified content.
Refer to detail information of content to fill input parameters.
If can't find thumbnail file for thumbnailId, thumbnail of no image will be returned.

parameters.name: capturedFileName
parameters.description: fileName composed with mac address of device to get captured image.

parameters.name: lastLoginDate
parameters.description: Needed for set response' cache Header(Expires), If null, will not caching on browser side.

parameters.name: logoFilePath
parameters.description: filePath including file name of custom logo image.

parameters.name: thumbnailId
parameters.description: Id of thumbnail or MOVIE_THUMBNAIL, OFFICE_THUMBNAIL, FLASH_THUMBNAIL, PDF_THUMBNAIL, SOUND_THUMBNAIL, ETC_THUMBNAIL, NOIMAGE_THUMBNAIL, STRM_THUMBNAIL, URL_THUMBNAIL, HTML_THUMBNAIL, SAPP_THUMBNAIL, FTP_THUMBNAIL, CIFS_THUMBNAIL, DLK_THUMBNAIL, MEDIASLIDE_THUMBNAIL are allowable. To download custom logo image, fill CUSTOM_LOGO as a thumbnailId and logoFilePath. Fill CAPTURE as a thumbnailId to get current captured image of device.

post.description:
You can get the output by combining "data" and "format".
In the case below, the first index and the second index are values ​​of data and type respectively, and the values ​​in parentheses are additional parameters required by the function.
Case:
- [data,format,(additional required parameters)]
- [countByType,periodTable]
- [countByDate,typeTable,(startDate,endDate)]
- [countByDate,typeChart,(startDate,endDate)]
- [detailReportOnGeneral,detailReportTable,(organizationId,year)]
- [detailReportOnVWL,detailReportTable,(organizationId,year)]
- [detailReportOnMedia,detailReportTable,(organizationId,year)]
- [detailReportOnEvent,detailReportTable,(organizationId,year)]
- [playFrequency,frequencyTable,(contentIds,groupIds,time,unit,startDate,endDate)]
- [detailStatisticsMediaReport,contentDetailReportTable,(organizationId,year)]
- [storageUsageByOrganization,(selectedOrganizationIds)]
- [storageUsageAll,(selectedOrganizationIds)]
- [popFileHistoryPeriods]
- [popFileHistory,historyChart,(searchMonth)]
- [popFileHistory,receivedChart,(startDate,pageSize,startIndex)]
- [popFileHistory,notReceivedChart,(startDate,pageSize,startIndex)]
Finally, in the case of "playFrequency", you need to specify the Unit for each Time type. For example, a unit of "yesterday" would be "Day".

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Resource for statistics of contents.Combination of "data" and "format" and some paramters. Refer to API description.

post.description:
You can export according to "data"
In the case below, the first index is the value of "data" and the values ​​in parentheses are additional parameters required by the function.
Case:
- [data,(additional required parameters)]
- [count,(startDate,endDate)]
- [playFrequency,(contentIds,groupIds,time,unit,startDate,endDate)]
- [storageUsages,(selectedOrganizationIds)]
- [popFileHistory,(searchMonth)]
Finally, for "playFrequency", you need to specify the units for each time type. For example, the "yesterday" unit is "Day".
